page: Microsoft Azure E4s_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the Esv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 2 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with an x86_64 architecture. Operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz, the processor includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and a large 480 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for remote disk attachments.

Performance benchmarks indicate a balance of average and weak results across various workloads. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is average at 2034.29, whereas multi-core performance is weak at 4104.86, reflecting the physical core limitation. Memory bandwidth tests show a weak read-only cached small block speed of 304.9 GB/sec and a weak uncached large block write speed of 22.69 GB/sec, though uncached large block reads perform at an average rate of 47.67 GB/sec. Single-threaded gzip compression is a notable strength, registering a strong throughput of 32.55 GB/sec, while multi-threaded bzip3 decompression remains weak at 26.16 GB/sec.

For specialized tasks, the processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for deep learning. In LLM inference tests using a small 135M model, the instance achieves an average prompt processing speed of 707.17 tokens per second and text generation of 174.84 tokens per second. However, performance drops to a poor 5.58 tokens per second for text generation on a medium 7B model. Database operations under Passmark are weak, though Redis SET operations maintain an average of 8.21 million operations per second.

Given its high memory density relative to compute resources, the E4s_v7 offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require heavy multi-core processing or local NVMe storage. It is best suited for relational databases, caching layers like Redis, medium-scale web serving, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure E4s_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Esv7 family, powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPUs, this instance relies on remote Premium Storage. It features a massive 480 MB L3 cache and supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instructions. Benchmark results show strong single-threaded gzip compression at 32.55 GB/sec, average single-core CPU performance, and average Redis SET throughput of 8.21 million operations per second, though multi-core scaling and database operations are relatively weak. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, low-to-moderate compute workloads such as caching layers, web serving, and relational databases.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E4s_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 480 MB L3 cache, designed for memory-intensive workloads using remote Premium Storage.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E4s_v7 is a memory-optimized VM with 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 480 MB L3 cache, optimized for remote Premium Storage.
tagline: An Intel-powered, memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high memory density and advanced matrix extensions for memory-bound enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Large 480 MB L3 cache with support for Intel AMX and AVX-512 instructions
Premium Storage capable configuration with no local storage or onboard GPUs
Strong single-threaded gzip compression performance of 32.55 GB/sec
Best suited for memory-bound workloads, caching layers, and relational databases
